segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a basketball hoop under bright arena lights, accompanied by a roaring crowd and a male announcer's enthusiastic "Hi." This transitions to a graphic showcasing the starting lineups for Lithuania and Hungary, complete with player names, numbers, and national flags, as the announcer expresses excitement "to get some NBA action going." The announcer then introduces the broadcast team, stating, "Alongside Chris Webber and Greg Anthony, I'm Kevin Harlan, the fourth member of our crew. From the sideline, Hall of Famer David Aldridge." He sets the stage for the game, announcing, "It'll be the visiting team, facing the home team." A whistle blows as the camera focuses on Jonas Valanciunas, Lithuania’s #17, in his green and yellow 'LIETUVA' and 'BIOVELA' jersey, bending and then standing, preparing for the game. The view then widens to show players from both teams at center court for the tip-off, surrounded by a packed, animated crowd, with the distinct sounds of basketball dribbling and continued crowd noise. The scoreboard displays 0-0 with 9:57 left in the first quarter as the announcer introduces, "Now the starting group for the visitors." Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession after the tip and dribbles up the court. Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, defends aggressively as the ball is passed around the perimeter, accompanied by the squeak of shoes and ongoing crowd sounds. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, drives toward the basket and scores a layup, giving Hungary a 2-0 lead. During this play, an announcer mentions, "Hopkins, guarded by Valanciunas," though the visual focus is on Allen's score. The camera closely follows the action, capturing defensive rotations and offensive movements, with players from both teams actively engaging. The court prominently features 'TCL', 'Smart', 'Tencent', and 'Tissot' branding, with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court. An announcer describes a shot, saying, "Rob... Fires top of the key. And a little luck that time, but it drops," which seems to refer to a different play than the visually depicted layup. The crowd remains lively, reacting to the play as the game progresses, with Lithuania’s #17, Valanciunas, and Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, visible in defensive stances. The scoreboard updates to 2-0 with 9:43 remaining, and Hungary maintains possession while Lithuania defends. Another announcer comments on the atmosphere, stating, "And that'll start us off already. You can feel the tension in this arena. Oh man, these fans are pumped. I expect it to be loud tonight. Should be a great game." The game continues with ongoing basketball sounds. An announcer then calls out, "Hopkins, defended by Sabonis," indicating another specific matchup on the court.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary, showing the tip-off with Lithuania leading 2-0 in the first quarter and 9:22 remaining. Prominent TCL and Colgate logos adorn the court, while Tissot and Smart advertising lines the sidelines. Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, is seen bending and standing before the tip. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession and dribbles up the court, with Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, defending aggressively. The camera captures close-ups of Benke and Grigonis's focused expressions. Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, then scores a layup, changing the score to Lithuania 2, Hungary 1. Immediately following, Benke is seen driving towards the basket, prompting a commentator to exclaim "driving in" before a "shooting foul" is called, accompanied by a distinct whistle sound. The commentator further explains the defensive strategy: "Really at the right play defensively, if you can't block, force him to the line." Benke, appearing determined, prepares for a free throw as an announcer declares, "At the line for your home team. Two shots." Another voice is heard repeating, "Take a break. Two shots." Benke takes the free throw, and the commentator confirms, "First free throw is good." The scoreboard updates to Hungary 2, and Benke claps his hands in satisfaction. The segment concludes with a wide view of the court as players reset for the next play, with the scoreboard still showing 9:22 remaining in the first quarter. The professional arena atmosphere is enhanced by bright lighting and visible crowd noise throughout the sequence.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the tip-off of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary, the score tied at 2-2 with 9:22 remaining in the first quarter. As the game begins, a commentator notes, "And so he hits both," followed by crowd applause, suggesting recent free throws. Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, in a green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, bends down before the tip, while Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, in a white jersey, gains possession. The court prominently displays a red 'TCL' logo and the colorful 'FIBA EuroBasket 2022' logo. Benke then dribbles up the court, passing to Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, who drives to the basket and scores a layup, giving Hungary a 4-2 lead. Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, then defends aggressively against Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, who attempts to drive. The ball is passed around the perimeter, with Hungary’s #9, David Vojvoda, in a defensive stance. A commentator exclaims, "To the middle. Here's Valanciunas. And it's Valanciunas finishing it off," as Jonas Valanciunas scores, with the commentator adding analysis about his "serious hops" despite being an "undersized center." A player shouts, "Screen, screen, screen!" during the action. The commentator describes a missed shot, stating, "Defended by Sabonis. Can't hit the free throw line jumper," and updates the time, "We've played just over a minute here in the first." A jump ball occurs near the basket, with the referee signaling 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS', a moment the commentator describes as "a bit of a battle here for the ball. The official signals a jump ball here." The camera briefly focuses on Vojvoda communicating with teammates. The game resumes with Hungary in possession, Benke dribbling again. The court is surrounded by various advertising banners for 'FIBA.basketball', 'TCL', 'molten', 'Ganten', and '百岁山', with 'COLOGNE' on the blue court area. The scoreboard updates to 8:45 remaining, showing Lithuania leading 4-2. The atmosphere is intense, with dynamic player movement and an engaged crowd. Upbeat, rhythmic background music begins, continuing as Benke dribbles near the top of the key, before the segment concludes with a distinct buzzer sound.

segments.integrated_caption: The opening minute of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary ignites in a packed arena, filled with the vibrant roar of the crowd. The game begins with a jump ball at center court, where Lithuania's Jonas Valanciunas (#17), in his green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, contests the tip against Hungary's Szilard Benke (#11), wearing a white jersey. A referee in a black-and-white striped shirt signals 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' as Benke secures possession and dribbles up the court. The polished wood surface of the court reflects bright arena lights, prominently displaying branding such as the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, TCL, Colgate, Smart, Tencent, Tissot, Ganten, and molten. Hungary quickly takes the lead as Rosco Allen (#5) drives to the basket and scores a layup, making it 2-0. Lithuania responds with a defensive stop, and after a turnover, Marius Grigonis (#40) drives and scores a layup, tying the game at 4-4. Hungary's Adam Hanga (#8) then scores a layup to put them ahead 5-4. The commentator notes a player being "guarded by Valanciunas" before Valanciunas himself, described as "firing the three" and "drilling it from outside" by the commentator, sinks a three-pointer to give Lithuania a 6-5 lead. The scoreboard updates in real-time, ticking down from 8:36 to 7:37 in the first quarter. Amidst the fast-paced action, the commentator mentions "Sabonis with the bucket," noting he "finishes strong" and is "comfortable shooting at that range." Later, a player named "Hopkins" is heard being "guarded by Valanciunas," who is also credited with securing a rebound. Hungary's David Vojvoda (#9) and Lithuania's Grigonis (#40) are seen actively defending. The minute concludes with Szilard Benke (#11) hitting a three-pointer, which the commentator describes as "canning it from downtown," giving Hungary an 8-6 lead, as fans in colorful attire react to every play.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures a dynamic opening minute of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 basketball game between Lithuania and Hungary, played in a packed arena with vibrant crowd energy. The game begins with a jump ball at center court, where Jonas Valanciunas (#17) for Lithuania and Szilard Benke (#11) for Hungary contest the tip. Benke secures the ball and dribbles up the court, initiating Hungary’s offense. The court features prominent branding, including the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, TCL center court logo, and advertisements for Colgate, Smart, Tencent, Tissot, Ganten, and molten. As play progresses, Hungary’s Rosco Allen (#5) drives to the basket and scores a layup, giving Hungary a 2-0 lead. Lithuania responds with a defensive stop, and after a turnover, Marius Grigonis (#40) drives and scores, tying the game at 4-4. Hungary regains possession, and Adam Hanga (#8) scores a layup to put Hungary ahead 5-4. Lithuania quickly answers with a three-pointer from Valanciunas, taking a 6-5 lead. The teams trade possessions, with Hungary’s David Vojvoda (#9) and Lithuania’s Grigonis (#40) actively defending. Hungary’s Benke (#11) then hits a three-pointer, giving Hungary an 8-6 lead. The camera follows the fast-paced action, capturing close-ups of players’ focused expressions and the referee’s signals, including a 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' call during the tip-off. The scoreboard updates in real-time, showing the clock ticking down from 8:36 to 7:37 in the first quarter, with the shot clock also visible. The atmosphere is electric, with fans in colorful attire reacting to each play, and the court’s polished wood surface reflecting the bright arena lights.

segments.integrated_caption: The dynamic opening minute of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania (in green and yellow) and Hungary (in white) kicks off with a jump ball at center court, where the referee signals 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS'. Szilard Benke (#11) of Hungary gains possession, dribbling upcourt past aggressive Lithuanian defender Marius Grigonis (#40). The ball moves around the perimeter as Hungary sets up their offense, leading to Rosco Allen (#5) driving for an early layup, giving Hungary a 2-0 lead. Lithuania responds with Jonas Valanciunas (#17) driving hard, drawing defenders before passing out. Amidst the squeaking shoes, ball dribbling, and animated crowd noise, commentators note "Sabonis trying to get open" and later "Sabonis with the steal," highlighting the Lithuanian player's activity. Hungary's Adam Hanga (#8) then scores a layup, extending their lead to 8-6, as the scoreboard prominently displays the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 and TCL logos, alongside ads for JUNCKERS, WANDA, Colgate, Ganten, and J9.com. The fast-paced action continues with defensive rotations and quick passes. Commentary identifies "Jokubaitis defended by Allen" during a possession where a shot "off the back iron and out" is missed, followed by analysis that "he takes advantage of open looks inside more often than not." A "pass to Hopkins" is also noted. With "six on the shot clock," a player "pulls up on the elbow" for a shot that is "a miss, O for one to start the contest." The sequence culminates with Benke hitting a three-pointer, reacting with satisfaction, as the scoreboard updates and the commentator remarks, "and that's exactly what he's looking for, draining the triple."

segments.integrated_caption: The first quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary opens with the score tied at 8-8, as the referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, signals 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' for the jump ball at center court. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession and dribbles up the court, which is prominently branded with logos like FIBA EuroBasket 2022, TCL, Colgate, molten, Ganten, and FIBA.basketball banners, alongside Smart, Tencent, Tissot, WANDA, and J9.com advertisements. Amidst the animated crowd noise and an upbeat background music, commentators discuss the game's dynamics; David Aldridge reports from the sideline about the home team coach's emphasis on defensive communication against an opportunistic opponent, a sentiment echoed by the main announcers who stress the need for a great defensive effort from all five players to slow down shooters and set the tone early. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, drives to the basket and scores a layup, giving Hungary a brief lead, while Lithuania responds with aggressive defense led by #40, Marius Grigonis, actively rotating and pressuring the perimeter. Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, then scores another layup, extending their lead to 10-8, with the scoreboard showing Lithuania at 4/8 (50%) and Hungary at 3/7 (43%) field goal percentages. The camera follows the fast-paced action, capturing close-ups of players like #9, David Vojvoda, and #17, Jonas Valanciunas, who drives to the basket, draws defenders, and passes out. The intense trading of possessions and defensive pressure culminates in a three-pointer by Benke, which an announcer calls "good" as it "just snugs right down through the net," bringing the score to 11-10 for Hungary, with Benke reacting with satisfaction. The commentators note this as a chance for Hungary to retake the lead.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the dynamic FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary in a packed, electric arena, as the first quarter clock ticks down from 5:40 to 4:45. The game begins with a jump ball at center court, where the referee signals 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' as Hungary's Szilard Benke (#11) gains possession. Hungary quickly advances, with Rosco Allen (#5) driving for a layup to take a 2-0 lead. The atmosphere is electric, with loud cheers and roars from fans in colorful attire, while commentators discuss previous plays and a player's early game performance. Lithuania responds, as Jonas Valanciunas (#17) aggressively drives, drawing defenders before passing out to a teammate who scores, tying the game at 10-10. Commentators highlight Valanciunas's impact, noting a 'rejection by Valanciunas!' on defense and later praising a player's 'passing with a purpose' that 'lead to scores.' Hungary's Adam Hanga (#8) then scores a layup to regain the lead, followed by Valanciunas hitting a mid-range jumper to tie again. The sounds of dribbling, squeaking shoes, and player calls fill the court as Marius Grigonis (#40) defends fiercely, rotating and contesting shots. Hungary extends their lead to 13-10 when Szilard Benke (#11) hits a three-pointer, with commentators exclaiming, 'Fires from deep. Connects from three-point range. He has five.' The teams continue to battle, with David Vojvoda (#9) actively defending. Later, a Lithuanian player identified by commentators as 'Milaknis' makes a shot, described as 'his second in five tries,' with praise for 'his hustle.' Prominent branding like FIBA EuroBasket 2022, TCL, Smart, Tencent, Tissot, Colgate, Ganten, JUNCKERS, FIBA.basketball, molten, WANDA, and J9.com are visible around the court, capturing the fast-paced action.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a jump ball at center court during a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game, with the scoreboard showing Lithuania leading Hungary 15-10 in the first quarter with 4:37 remaining. Prominent court branding like 'TCL', 'FIBA EuroBasket 2022', 'JUNCKERS', 'WANDA', 'Ganten', and 'Colgate' is visible. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession, dribbling up the court as Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, aggressively defends. Commentators discuss Hungary's strategy of "not settling" and going "right to the rim." The play transitions into a fast break, with Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, driving to the basket and scoring a layup. Commentators note a pass "to Hanga" and the need "to halt the run." Valanciunas is then seen rebounding, with commentators highlighting that he "has got four rebounds in this game." Lithuania responds with a strong offensive push; Valanciunas drives hard, drawing defenders, and passes out to a teammate, identified by commentators as Jokubaitis, who banks in a mid-range jumper, extending Lithuania’s lead to 17-10. Commentators analyze the play, noting the team is "one step ahead" and "gaining some confidence," while criticizing the "lack of rim protection" and "too many high percentage looks" given up. Defensive rotations by Marius Grigonis (#40) and Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, are captured. After the score, Hungary calls a timeout, and the scene shifts to a court-side entertainment segment. Upbeat, rhythmic electronic music plays as cheerleaders in black crop tops and shorts perform a synchronized routine, tossing basketballs into the air. The crowd, a mix of fans in various colors, watches intently. The scoreboard remains visible, frozen at 4:09 during the timeout, with Hungary’s possession clock ticking down. The atmosphere is electric, with bright arena lights and dynamic camera movements.

segments.integrated_caption: The FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds in a packed arena, with the scoreboard showing Lithuania leading 17-10 in the first quarter. An energetic electronic music track, accompanied by rhythmic clapping and the roar of the animated crowd, sets an electric atmosphere. An announcer's voice exclaims, "Get ready for the dance squad!" as the camera cuts to a close-up of the Hungarian team, wearing white jerseys with red and green trim, huddled around their coach during a timeout, labeled 'TIEMPO DE HUNGARY'. Four cheerleaders in black crop tops and shorts perform a synchronized routine, tossing basketballs, highlighted by bright stage lights, while the coach, seated and gesturing with a clipboard, delivers instructions to players like #11 Szilard Benke, #8 Adam Hanga, #6 Keller, #20 Perl, #25 Varadi, and #15 Ferencz, who listen intently. After the timeout, the game resumes on a court adorned with prominent branding including the FIBA EuroBasket 2022, TCL, and Nike logos, alongside sideboard advertisements for Smart, Tencent, Tissot, Colgate, FIBA.basketball, molten, Ganten, JUNCKERS, WANDA, and J9.com. The ball is tipped off, signaled by a referee in a black-and-white striped shirt, and Hungary gains possession. Szilard Benke dribbles up the court, passing to Rosco Allen (#5), who drives to the basket and scores a layup, met with continued crowd noise and game sounds like dribbling and squeaking shoes. Lithuania’s Jonas Valanciunas (#17) and Marius Grigonis (#40) defend aggressively. Another announcer's voice announces a "Substitution for Lithuania." The sequence culminates with Hungary’s #11 Benke hitting a three-pointer, reacting with satisfaction, while Lithuania’s defense rotates, with Grigonis contesting shots and Valanciunas setting screens and shooting a mid-range jumper, which a commentator notes as "No good, unable to end this run," adding, "They've been beating them..." before the audio cuts.

segments.integrated_caption: The first quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary in Cologne opens with Lithuania leading 17-10, as commentators discuss the importance of securing "loose balls and rebounds." A jump ball at center court, signaled by the referee with 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS', sees Hungary's Szilard Benke (#11) gain possession. The fast-paced action unfolds with Hungary's Rosco Allen (#5) driving to the basket for a layup, followed by Adam Hanga (#8) also scoring a layup. Lithuania responds, with Jonas Valanciunas (#17), wearing his green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, driving aggressively, drawing defenders, and passing out. Commentators highlight a missed shot by a player identified as "Kukevicius" or "Lukevicius" that "will not go," hitting "off the front iron." Valanciunas then makes a powerful defensive play, executing a "rejection" and "recovering" the ball, as the animated crowd buzzes with murmurs and cheers, punctuated by the squeak of shoes and the thud of the basketball. The score is noted as a "seven-point spread." segments.integrated_caption: The first quarter of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds in a packed, vibrant arena, filled with the sounds of an engaged crowd. The action begins with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gaining possession after the tip, dribbling up the court under aggressive defense from Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas. The court prominently displays the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, the red TCL logo, and sideboard ads for JUNCKERS, WANDA, Ganten, molten, J9.com, and Colgate. Commentary notes a pass to 'Brazdeikis,' followed by a deep shot that 'drops in the tray,' with a second commentator reflecting on how often they see 'a possession like that... ending with a basket coming off a pretty pass.' Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, drives to the basket and scores a layup, giving Hungary the first points. Lithuania responds with a fast break, where Valanciunas drives hard, drawing defenders, and passing out to a teammate who scores, extending Lithuania’s lead to 20-12. A shot from 12 feet is then attempted but declared 'no good, trying to use the glass.' The camera closely follows the ball, capturing defensive rotations by Marius Grigonis (#40) and Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, who later scores a layup. The atmosphere remains intense with rhythmic crowd sounds and chants. As the shot clock ticks down, the ball is 'gets it to Valanciunas' with 'five to shoot.' Valanciunas then hits a mid-range jumper, with the commentator exclaiming, 'And he hits it just before the shot clock expires!' This increases Lithuania’s lead to 22-12, as the clock shows 2:06 remaining in the first quarter, and the commentator confirms, 'Valanciunas has got his second bucket of the game to go.' The court's branding, including TCL, Ganten, and J9.com banners, along with the 'COLOGNE' text, remains consistently visible.

And the last shot before the buzzer is off," as the scoreboard displays 'FIN DEL 1.ER C.'. Lithuania’s #13 celebrates with a clenched fist, while Hungary’s #25 appears frustrated, and Lithuania’s #31 roars in triumph, emphasizing the team’s momentum. The scene transitions to the start of the second quarter, marked by 'INICIO DEL 2.° C.' and 'Sustituciones' text, with players from both teams seen on the bench and court. The commentator highlights, "They're up by 10. And the second quarter about to get started. We'll be back in just a moment." Upbeat, energetic background music begins, accompanying visuals of cheerleaders in black outfits performing a synchronized routine, tossing basketballs into the air under bright stage lights, signaling a break. A referee in a black-and-white striped shirt monitors the play, while a whoosh sound effect and a distinct "ding" are heard during this interlude. As the commentators return, one welcomes back viewers to the second quarter, noting, "Plenty of basketball left to play, but this one has been one-sided so far." Another asks for thoughts on the visiting team, to which a third responds, "Well, this is the edge that great rim protection can give you," before the audio fades.

segments.integrated_caption: The second quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary commences with Lithuania leading 26-16, as the court prominently displays the colorful FIBA EuroBasket 2022 and red TCL logos. On-screen text 'Sustituciones' appears as players from both teams enter, followed by a brief cheerleader performance with basketballs, all while a commentator notes, "The shooters look frustrated seeing so many of their shots get stifled," punctuated by a buzzer. The game resumes with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gaining possession after the tip and dribbling up the court. Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, drives aggressively, drawing defenders and passing out, with a commentator mentioning a "Pass to Kuzminskas." Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, then drives and scores a layup, followed by Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, also scoring a layup amidst the sounds of dribbling and squeaking shoes. Lithuania responds with a defensive stop, and #17 Valanciunas hits a mid-range jumper. The fast-paced play continues with defensive rotations by Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, and Hungary’s #9, David Vojvoda, as the scoreboard updates. A commentator calls out "Bukevicius," followed by "No good on the triple," eliciting a mixed crowd reaction, and later notes a shot is "wide right, hits off the rim." Advertisements for Smart, Tencent, Colgate, FIBA.basketball, Ganten, and J9.com are visible along the sidelines. A referee in a black-and-white striped shirt closely monitors the game, signaling 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' during a jump ball. The animated crowd and electric atmosphere underscore the players' intense focus and athleticism. The sequence culminates with a close-up of a blocked shot, highlighted by the 'Mobil 1 TAPONES' graphic, as Lithuania’s #17 Valanciunas contests a shot near the basket, with a commentator identifying "Lukevicius" and "Kuzminskas" during play, then announcing "A waiting foul. First personal foul," before highlighting the "Mobile One block, defensive performance" with a replay.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, in his green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, executing a powerful shot block near the basket, which triggers a 'Mobil 1 TAPONES' graphic overlay. A male commentator notes that such plays "might serve as a spark for this team." The scene then transitions to a wide-angle view of a jump ball at center court, where the referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, signals 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' as Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, in a white jersey, gains possession amidst the animated crowd's sounds. An announcer is heard stating, "Substitution for the away team," though the 'Sustituciones' text appears visually later. The scoreboard shows Lithuania leading 26-16 in the second quarter with 9:17 remaining. Hungary advances the ball, with on-court player shouts like "Go, go, go!" and "Come on, man!" audible, accompanied by dribbling and squeaking shoes. Rosco Allen (#5) drives for a layup, followed by Adam Hanga (#8) also converting a layup, narrowing the gap. Lithuania responds with a strong offensive sequence led by Valanciunas, who drives into the paint, draws defenders, and passes to Lithuania’s #13, who celebrates with a clenched fist after a successful play. The court prominently features branding like the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, TCL center court logo, and sideboard ads for Tissot, Tencent, Ganten, and JUNCKERS. As the quarter progresses, Hungary’s #25 appears frustrated during a defensive stand, while Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, aggressively defends the perimeter. A commentator highlights "Four on the shot clock" before a missed shot, adding "No good there, and that would have cut it to single digits." Another commentator praises a "nice defensive play to disrupt the alley-oop." The action shifts back and forth, with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, hitting a three-pointer and reacting with satisfaction. The court’s blue and white design, with 'COLOGNE' written near the basket, remains consistent, and the crowd's cheers and reactions intensify, building anticipation. The segment concludes with Lithuania’s #31 roaring in triumph after a successful basket, confirmed by a referee's whistle and a commentator stating, "basket is good." The scoreboard updates to 26-18, and the 'Sustituciones' text appears, signaling a transition to the second quarter.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Hungary’s #14, GOLOMAN, in a white jersey with red and green trim, visibly frustrated after a missed shot, wiping his face and gesturing in disappointment, while commentators discuss a "chance here for a three-point play" and praise a player as "one of the best... when it comes to making that lead pass." An announcer then calls out "At the line for your home team!" and "One plus the penalty, two shots," followed by the distinct sound of a basketball swishing, suggesting successful free throws. The camera then cuts to Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, in a green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, soaring for a powerful two-handed dunk, the ball swishing through the net, though a commentator exclaims "Oh, deep three-pointer!" immediately after a swish sound. A commentator notes "A minute and a half gone here in the second quarter" as the scene transitions to a wide court view, with GOLOMAN looking dejected at center court. The scoreboard shows Lithuania leading 26-18 with 8:37 remaining in the second quarter. The game resumes with GOLOMAN receiving the ball at the top of the key to inbound, passing to #11, Szilard Benke, who dribbles up the court amidst general game sounds. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, defends aggressively, forcing a turnover. The ball is recovered by Lithuania’s #30, who drives into the paint, drawing defenders and passing to #17, Jonas Valanciunas, who hits a mid-range jumper, extending Lithuania’s lead. The crowd reacts with cheers and general game noise, and the camera pans across the court, featuring prominent branding like 'TCL', 'molten', 'Ganten', 'J9.com', and the 'FIBA EuroBasket 2022' logo at center court. The brightly lit arena is filled with spectators as the sequence ends with players resetting, Lithuania maintaining control.

segments.integrated_caption: The FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds with Lithuania leading 26-19 in the second quarter, a period the announcer notes has seen "two minutes gone by." Hungary's #11, Szilard Benke, initiates play after a tip-off, dribbling up the court under aggressive defense from Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis. An early shot attempt is heard, with the announcer calling out "shot clock at five," then "from downtown," followed by "that one's off, he starts the game with a miss." The camera tracks the fast-paced action across the court, adorned with prominent branding like the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, TCL, Ganten, Smart, Tencent, and Colgate. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, drives powerfully to the basket, drawing defenders, and scores a dunk, which the announcer confirms as "good with the extra effort on the glass," pushing Lithuania's lead to 28-19 on the real-time scoreboard. The energetic crowd reacts with cheers. Hungary quickly responds with a three-pointer from Benke, narrowing the score to 28-21. Following a successful play, Lithuania’s #13 celebrates with a clenched fist. The sequence continues with Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, driving to the basket, drawing defenders, and passing out to a teammate who scores a mid-range jumper, described by the announcer as "the layup's good off the glass." The vibrant court design, including the colorful FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo and Nike swoosh, enhances the visual intensity. A referee in a black-and-white striped shirt closely monitors the play, while the bright lighting consistently illuminates the players and detailed court graphics. The electric atmosphere is palpable, with fans in team colors reacting to each play. The segment concludes with Hungary’s #14, Goloman, receiving the ball at center court to inbound, appearing focused, as Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, defends aggressively, showcasing his prowess. On-screen graphics continuously display the score, time, and shot clock.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures a dynamic sequence from a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 basketball game between Lithuania (LIT) and Hungary (HUN), with Lithuania leading 26-19 in the second quarter. The action begins with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, in possession after a tip-off, dribbling up the court under pressure from Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, who aggressively defends. The camera follows the fast-paced play as players move across the court, which features prominent branding including the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, TCL court logo, and advertisements for Ganten, Smart, Tencent, and Colgate. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, drives hard to the basket, drawing defenders, and scores a powerful dunk, increasing Lithuania’s lead to 28-19. The scoreboard updates in real-time, and the crowd reacts with energy. Hungary responds with a three-pointer from Benke, narrowing the gap to 28-21. The ball is then passed to Lithuania’s #13, who celebrates with a clenched fist after a successful play. The sequence continues with Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, driving to the basket, drawing defenders, and passing out to a teammate who scores a mid-range jumper. The court’s vibrant design, including the colorful FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo and Nike swoosh, adds to the visual intensity. The referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, closely monitors the play, signaling fouls and maintaining order. The lighting remains bright and consistent, highlighting the players’ movements and the detailed court graphics. The atmosphere is electric, with fans in the stands wearing team colors and reacting to each play. segments.integrated_caption: The FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary, with Lithuania leading 28-21 in the second quarter, unfolds with intense action and dynamic commentary. The segment begins with Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, driving aggressively toward the basket, drawing defenders before passing out, a play that prompts a commentator to praise a "great job of screening" and taking it "to the rim and get the finish." Hungary’s #14, Goloman, receives the ball at center court and attempts a shot, contested by Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis. After a missed shot, Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, secures the rebound and initiates a fast break, leading to a layup, followed by a whistle sound and a commentator noting the "first turnover of the game." The scoreboard updates to 7:05 remaining in the quarter, and a "substitution" is announced as the camera briefly shows the Lithuanian bench, where players like #17 Jonas Valanciunas react with focus. The game resumes with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, dribbling up the court, passing to #14 Goloman, who drives and attempts a shot that is emphatically blocked by Lithuania’s #19 Zemaitis. This defensive effort earns commendation from a commentator for "great defense" and having "anticipated the play and got there first." The crowd, visible in red, green, and white, is engaged, with advertising banners for Tencent, Tissot, JUNCKERS, WANDA, and Ganten, along with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 and TCL logos, prominently displayed. A referee in a black-and-white striped shirt closely monitors the play. Lithuania recovers the ball, and #19 Zemaitis drives again, drawing defenders and passing to #13, who scores a mid-range jumper. The audio then highlights a "triple" attempt that "misses that one," followed by another "right block shot" by Goloman that is "blocked" again, showcasing Zemaitis's athleticism. Hungary’s #14 Goloman is visibly frustrated after his missed shots, wiping his face. The sequence concludes with the ball being passed to "Sabonis" as the "clock at six," maintaining the intense atmosphere with real-time scoreboard updates.

segments.integrated_caption: The second quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds with Lithuania holding a 31-21 lead, as the scoreboard ticks down from 6:36 to 5:36. The action begins with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gaining possession after a tip-off and advancing the ball up the court. Early in the segment, commentary notes a successful "three-pointer," stating "for the three, trains the three-pointer," with the announcer remarking that "without looking at the scoreboard, you think they were the team trailing." This likely refers to a three-pointer later hit by Benke, who is seen reacting with satisfaction. The camera follows the fast-paced offensive and defensive transitions, highlighting Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, who drives aggressively to the basket, drawing defenders, and then passes out to set up a play. Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, is seen actively defending, rotating to contest shots and applying pressure on Hungary’s perimeter players. Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, and #9, David Vojvoda, are visible in defensive stances, attempting to disrupt Lithuania’s rhythm. The court features prominent branding including TCL, molten, Ganten, J9.com, and FIBA.basketball banners, with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo and TCL court logo clearly visible at center court. Commentary notes "just over three and a half minutes played" in the quarter. As play progresses, Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, executes a powerful dunk, energizing the crowd, while Lithuania’s #31 roars in triumph after a successful basket. A missed three-point attempt is heard, with the commentator stating, "Tried to come right back with a three of his own, but it's no good." Hungary’s #14, Goloman, appears frustrated after a missed shot, wiping his face, and later receives the ball at center court to inbound. The referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, closely monitors the play, signaling 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' during the jump ball and maintaining control throughout. The crowd is animated, with fans in red, green, and white attire reacting to the action, their cheers and the squeak of shoes audible throughout. Commentary highlights Lithuania's "big lead, not just on the scoreboard, but really in the rebounding numbers as well." Towards the end of the minute, commentator introduces "Kuzminskis," noting "He's still scoreless so far in this one," before he "lets the three fly," which "misses." The lighting remains bright and consistent, emphasizing the high-energy atmosphere of the arena. The sequence ends with Hungary attempting to mount a comeback, but Lithuania’s defense, led by Grigonis and Zemaitis, remains solid, showcasing their dominance in the quarter.

segments.integrated_caption: The second quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds with Lithuania leading 36-23. Commentary opens discussing a three-point shot and the critical error of leaving a shooter open beyond the arc. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession and dribbles up court, aggressively defended by Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis. The broadcast then introduces Hall of Famer David Aldrich, the fourth member of the crew, who greets 'Kevin' and discusses the NBA's diversity, noting frustration regarding head coach and front office positions. Aldrich quotes All-Star Karl-Anthony Towns, emphasizing that men of color deserve a chance to lead teams and organizations. Visually, Hungary briefly shifts momentum as #5, Rosco Allen, drives for a layup, followed by #8, Adam Hanga, also scoring a layup. Lithuania quickly responds with a strong offensive push: #17, Jonas Valanciunas, drives into the paint, drawing defenders before passing out to Lithuania’s #31, who hits a mid-range jumper, extending their lead to 38-23. The court is adorned with prominent branding from TCL, Tissot, Colgate, Ganten, WANDA, and JUNCKERS, with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court. The animated crowd's cheers are audible, and a referee closely monitors the play, having signaled 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' earlier. Commentators note both teams making changes and Lithuania building a significant lead that shows no signs of slowing down. segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the dynamic atmosphere of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game in Cologne, featuring Lithuania (green/yellow) and Hungary (white), with prominent court branding like TCL, molten, Ganten, and J9.com visible. While the visual action begins with a referee signaling 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' for a jump ball, the audio immediately captures ongoing play, with Hungary's Adam Hanga (#8) passing to Hopkins. The crowd is animated, and game sounds fill the arena as commentators highlight a successful "bucket," noting it as the player's third of the game. Analysts critique the "interior defense" as "too slow to protect the rim," a general observation that aligns with Lithuania's Marius Grigonis (#40) aggressively defending and rotating. Hungary's #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession after the tip and dribbles up court, but Lithuania forces a turnover. Lithuania transitions quickly, with #19, Zemaitis, driving to the basket for a powerful dunk, energizing the crowd. The scoreboard shows Lithuania leading 38-23 in the second quarter with 3:48 remaining. Following this, Jonas Valanciunas (#17) receives a pass and hits a mid-range jumper, extending the lead to 40-25. During other sequences, a pass goes to Kuzminskas, who is then seen defended by Hungary's Rosco Allen (#5) as the shot clock ticks down to five. After an offensive rebound, Kuzminskas tries another shot, and the ball is later passed to Sabonis. Commentators praise the team's "effort and aggression in the paint," noting their "massive" rebounding edge "right from the tip" as a successful play finally culminates in a score. Hungary responds to Lithuania's scoring with a three-pointer by Benke, who reacts with satisfaction. The sequence concludes with Hungary’s #14, Goloman, appearing frustrated after a missed shot and receiving the ball at center court to inbound.

segments.integrated_caption: The second quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary is intense, with Lithuania leading 40-25, later 42-27, and the clock ticking from 2:50 to 1:59. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession after a tip-off, driving to the basket against aggressive defense from Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas. The ball moves around the perimeter as Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, and #19, Zemaitis, rotate defensively. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, attempts a drive, with the commentator noting "Allen trying to break free, lays it up and gets it in," confirming his score and that "He's got nine." This leads to a turnover. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, then drives hard to the basket, drawing defenders and finishing with a powerful dunk, met with an emphatic "Boom, he jams it straight down" from the commentator, energizing the animated crowd in red and green. The scoreboard updates, expanding Lithuania’s lead. Commentary follows, analyzing the turnover: "Well, if you don't take care of the ball, fellas, that's what can happen. Absolutely great. That makes the turnover even more painful," and praising the quick transition: "going defense to offense in an instant. Every team now calibrated to do that." Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, responds with a layup. Lithuania’s #17, Valanciunas, hits a mid-range jumper to extend the lead further. The commentator also mentions "Lekavicius" and that "That three off the mark," referring to Lukas Lekavicius's missed shot. Prominent court branding includes TCL, FIBA EuroBasket 2022, Smart, Tencent, Tissot, Colgate, Ganten, molten, JUNCKERS, WANDA, and J9.com. The referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, closely monitors the play, signaling fouls and out-of-bounds. On-screen graphics display player stats and points scored off turnovers, while the atmosphere remains highly focused and energetic.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with an intense basketball match between Lithuania and Hungary in the second quarter, Lithuania leading 42-27, amidst the sounds of dribbling, squeaking shoes, and a murmuring crowd. Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, drives to the basket, drawing defenders before passing out to a teammate who scores, increasing Lithuania’s lead to 44-27. Following this, commentators describe "a missed layup" before highlighting "Sabonis with the steal," noting "he gets it to go" and that "Sabonis has got six in the quarter," while on-screen graphics later confirm Domantas Sabonis's 8 points. The commentary continues, with analysts discussing "a great job of just getting into the middle of that defense and really scoring effectively from the paint," and the need for "some answers offensively," criticizing "bad shot selection" and "bad luck" that "isn't gonna help you score points." The camera follows fast-paced offensive play, capturing Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, defending aggressively, and Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gaining possession and dribbling up the court. After a brief defensive sequence, Hungary calls a timeout, marked by a whistle and the transition to an upbeat, rhythmic electronic music track. The scene shifts to a vibrant cheerleading performance by four women in black crop tops and shorts, tossing basketballs into the air under bright stage lights, with the scoreboard displaying 'TIEMPO DE HUNGARY' and the clock frozen at 1:34. The crowd, a colorful mix of fans, watches intently. As the music fades and a whistle blows, the game resumes with the sounds of renewed basketball action. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, drives for a layup, and Hungary’s #8, Adam Hanga, scores another layup to narrow the gap. The court prominently features branding from TCL, Nike, J9.com, WANDA, and FIBA EuroBasket 2022, alongside Smart, Tencent, Tissot, Colgate, FIBA.basketball, molten, Ganten, and JUNCKERS. The atmosphere remains electric, with the crowd reacting to each play, and the lighting shifts to highlight the cheerleaders during the timeout before returning to the high-stakes gameplay.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with commentators reacting to a powerful play, with one exclaiming, "pass up strong for the slam!" and another adding, "Oh, power flush with a long hang on the rim," suggesting a preceding high-impact dunk that could be a "catalyst." The visual then shifts to a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary, with Lithuania leading 44-29 in the second quarter. Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, is seen driving aggressively, drawing defenders, and passing out. Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, actively defends and rotates. With 1:15 left in the first half, a commentator announces a significant defensive play: "And it's sent back by Allen!" followed by an announcer declaring, "First personal foul, first team foul." The game transitions to a free-throw situation, with the announcer stating, "Shooting for the away team... At the line for two." Despite the 'away team' announcement, the visual shows Valanciunas, Lithuania’s #17, stepping up to the line, taking a deep breath, and calmly sinking the shot. A commentator confirms, "The free throw drops for Valanciunas," extending Lithuania’s lead to 45-29. The vibrant crowd, a mix of red, green, and white, reacts with energy. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, walks back on defense with a focused expression, while Lithuania’s #13 celebrates with a clenched fist. The court features prominent branding like 'TCL', 'FIBA.basketball', 'molten', 'Ganten', and 'J9.com', with the 'FIBA EuroBasket 2022' logo and 'COLOGNE' branding visible at center court as players reset for the next possession. The referee closely monitors the intense atmosphere, with crowd noise and cheers audible throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a jump ball at center court during a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary, with Lithuania leading 45-29 in the second quarter. The court prominently displays a red TCL logo at center, surrounded by the colorful FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, while various advertisements like Smart, Tencent, Tissot, Colgate, Ganten, Nike, FIBA.basketball, molten, JUNCKERS, WANDA, and J9.com line the sidelines. Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, wearing a green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, secures the tip amidst the general game sounds and crowd noise, beginning the offensive possession. He drives to the basket, drawing defenders, and passes out to a teammate as the camera follows the fast-paced action. An announcer notes a successful play, stating, "And that's good as he hits both of his shots." Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession after a defensive rebound and dribbles up the court, attempting a three-pointer that misses. An announcer then states, "1:06 left to play in the first half," while the animated crowd begins a rhythmic chant. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, aggressively defends and later performs a powerful dunk, energizing the crowd. The scoreboard updates to 48-29 for Lithuania after a successful mid-range jumper by Valanciunas. The crowd's energy peaks as an announcer exclaims, "And the rejection by Valanciunas!" confirming a powerful block. Upbeat, rhythmic background music begins, accompanying the ongoing play. Another announcer mentions a pass to "DeBrons Dikas" going inside, with commentary praising a "money lead pass." Hungary’s #14, Goloman, appears frustrated after a missed shot, wiping his face, and later receives the ball at center court to inbound. The sequence concludes with Lithuania maintaining control, showcasing strong coordination, as an announcer confirms, "There's 42 seconds left to play here in the second quarter," with the bright lighting highlighting the polished court and vibrant branding.

segments.integrated_caption: The dynamic second quarter of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds, with Lithuania leading 48-29, set against an intense atmosphere with an animated crowd and background music. The action begins with a commentator noting, "The pass to Keller." Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, wearing a green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, drives aggressively to the basket, drawing defenders, and is then audibly identified by a commentator during a powerful "rejection by Valanciunas!" The commentators confirm, "They retain possession. They recover it." Upbeat background music plays as a commentator announces, "There's 21 seconds left in the first half of basketball." The fast-paced transition continues as Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession and dribbles up the court. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, scores a layup, but Lithuania’s defense, led by Marius Grigonis (#40), remains active, contesting shots and rotating effectively. With "Just five on the clock," a commentator identifies "Lukevicius," who then commits a "travel," confirmed by a referee's whistle and a commentator's call of "Team ball!" The scoreboard shows the clock ticking down. "Six seconds left here in the second," a commentator informs, followed by "Here's Keller." A buzzer sounds, signaling the end of the quarter, with the commentator adding, "And through one half, it hasn't even..." The commentator then mentions "18" before announcing, "We're going to step away briefly, but get you right back out here for the start of the third quarter after our halftime," as background music plays. During this break, the on-screen text 'DESCANSO' appears. Lithuania’s #13 celebrates with a clenched fist after a successful offensive move, while Lithuania’s #31 roars in triumph after a successful basket. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, is seen defending aggressively and later performing a powerful dunk. Hungary’s #14, Goloman, is visibly frustrated after a missed shot, wiping his face before receiving the ball at center court to inbound. Hungary’s players, including #9 David Vojvoda and #15, appear focused and determined. The court features prominent branding including 'TCL', 'FIBA EuroBasket 2022', 'JUNCKERS', 'WANDA', and 'J9.com', with 'molten' and 'Ganten' banners visible along the sidelines. The referee closely monitors the play, and the bright, consistent lighting highlights the polished wooden court and vibrant team colors as players from both teams walk off the court.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, in his green and yellow 'LIETUVA' and 'BIOVELA' jersey, walking off the court during a break, accompanied by upbeat, electronic background music. A futuristic 'DESCANSO DEL PARTIDO' (Halftime Break) graphic, presented by 2K, then appears with a distinct whoosh sound effect and a male voiceover stating, "A 2K Sports halftime." The scene transitions to a virtual NBA 2K studio where three animated commentators are seated behind a desk against a backdrop of red and blue geometric patterns. A male voice, identified as Ernie Johnson, welcomes viewers, saying, "Welcome back to 2K Sports, everybody," before introducing the panel: "Ernie Johnson, Shaquille O'Neal, Kenny the Jet Smith. Let's talk about that first half." The broadcast returns to the brightly lit court, featuring prominent 'molten', 'TCL', 'FIBA.basketball', and 'Tissot' branding, with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, in a white jersey, dribbles up the court and scores a three-pointer, reacting with satisfaction. Ernie then prompts, "Checking out the visiting team. Kenny, give us your take." Kenny 'The Jet' Smith provides analysis, stating, "Their passing was clearly superior in the first half. They were playing a team game, not one-on-one basketball. Good player movement, good ball movement, and they have a healthy lead to show for it." During this, Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, drives to the basket, drawing defenders, passes out, and then hits a mid-range jumper, while Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, defends aggressively, rotating to contest shots. Ernie then addresses Shaquille O'Neal, saying, "And big fella, let's get your thoughts on the home team." Shaquille O'Neal responds, "They need to give more energy and effort on the boards. You barely dealt with the main issues. Probably they're getting bullied. I'm not sure if they have the players to change that." The scoreboard shows Lithuania leading 48-29 at halftime, with Valanciunas leading in points (6) and rebounds (8), and Benke having 7 points for Hungary. The commentators reappear briefly, with the leftmost gesturing animatedly, before Ernie concludes, "And that's a wrap. With the third quarter approaching, we now send you back to Kevin and the crew." The 'DESCANSO DEL PARTIDO' graphic reappears, accompanied by the same upbeat electronic music and whoosh sound effect, signaling the end of the segment.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute begins with a black screen, transitioning to the vibrant FIBA EuroBasket 2022 arena, where Lithuania leads Hungary 48-29 at the start of the third quarter. Upbeat, dramatic music plays as a male announcer notes the "lopsided first half" and hopes for a more "evenly" played second half. The court is adorned with the colorful FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, a large red TCL logo, and a Nike swoosh, surrounded by advertising banners for Ganten, FIBA.basketball, Smart, Colgate, and TCL. Cheerleaders in black outfits perform a synchronized routine, tossing basketballs under bright stage lights, while the scoreboard displays 'INICIO DEL 3.ER C.' and 'Sustituciones'. The game resumes with basketball dribbling and squeaking shoes as Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession and dribbles up the court, defended by Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis. Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, drives to the basket, drawing defenders before passing out. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, then drives and scores a layup, updating the score to 48-32. A whistle blows, and the announcer calls out "Sabonis against Allen," followed by Allen sinking a three-pointer, bringing his total to "eight points for him." Another male announcer then provides context on Domantas Sabonis, mentioning he "averaged five assists per game, a crazy number for a center" during a breakout season. The first announcer declares "Third quarter action getting rolling right now, brought to you by Gatorade," and announces "Here's who's on the floor." Lithuania responds with a fast break, and #13 scores, celebrating with a clenched fist, updating the scoreboard to 51-32. Hungary’s #14, Goloman, looking frustrated after a missed shot, receives the ball at center court to inbound. The referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, monitors play closely. The ball is then passed to Hopkins, who successfully makes "a three-ball," with the announcer exclaiming, "A three-pointer is right on target!" The second announcer critiques the defense, stating, "They didn't do a good enough job on him. He could be an ace when he gets a good look at three." On-screen graphics display player names and substitutions throughout the electric atmosphere, with a packed crowd in colorful attire and dynamic lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The intense FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary sees Lithuania leading 53-32 in the third quarter. The action kicks off with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, driving aggressively before passing to #5, Rosco Allen, who scores a layup, narrowing the deficit to 53-34. The atmosphere is charged with crowd noise as commentators describe the next play: the ball is "dishe[d] to Valanciunas," Lithuania’s #17, who then drives "inside" and goes "strong with the one-handed jam." Commentators laud it as "just a simple one-hand flush," noting he "got a few more inches on his vertical by going with the spike." Following this powerful play, Lithuania’s #13 celebrates with a clenched fist. The game continues with a commentator mentioning a "pass to Hopkins." Hungary’s #9, David Vojvoda, attempts a mid-range shot that misses, and Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, secures the rebound. Another successful "elbow shot" is noted by commentators as "all good," marking a player's fifth basket of the game, now shooting "five for eleven." Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, then receives a pass and executes a powerful dunk, further energizing the engaged crowd. The fast-paced back-and-forth continues with players actively rotating on defense and offense, while the crowd can be heard chanting "Defense!" Commentators then announce "Sabonis with the ball," noting he "has eight" points. Later, Valanciunas is heard again, as he "finds to free himself up, and he gets it back." The sequence concludes with Hungary’s #14, Goloman, visibly frustrated after an earlier missed shot, wiping his face as he receives the ball at center court to inbound. Throughout, the referee in a black-and-white striped shirt closely monitors the action, and the court prominently displays branding from TCL, Smart, Tencent, Tissot, JUNCKERS, and FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logos. On-screen graphics update in real time, including a 'LÍDERES DE ANOTACIÓN' graphic highlighting top scorers R. Giedraitis (12 pts) and D. Vojvoda (11 pts).

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with commentary noting a missed finish, as Lithuania, leading 57-36 in the third quarter of a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game against Hungary, maintains a dynamic offensive flow. The camera follows Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, driving aggressively to the basket, drawing defenders and passing out. Commentary highlights a player taking it inside for a score, now shooting six for twelve, and being three for four from the field since halftime, reflecting Lithuania's efficient play. The court is brightly lit, showcasing prominent branding including TCL, Ganten, WANDA, J9.com, and molten, with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court. The crowd is animated, and a referee in black-and-white stripes monitors the action. The play continues with a pass to Domantas Sabonis (#11), who is double-teamed before executing a powerful slam dunk, drawing enthusiastic commentary praising his 'easy money' move and ability to 'throw it down' from deep. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession and dribbles up the court. Rosco Allen (#5) receives a 'dish' from a teammate, and later drives for a layup, scoring for Hungary, despite a subsequent three-point attempt by another player being off target, which commentary attributes to a 'defensive breakdown.' Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, defends actively against Hungary’s perimeter players, rotating and contesting shots. The scoreboard updates reflect Lithuania’s dominance, with Domantas Sabonis (#11) also scoring a mid-range jumper, highlighted by an on-screen graphic showing his stats: 10 points, 10 points in the paint. The segment concludes with commentary on a 'well-deserved assist,' as Lithuania maintains control, showcasing their offensive and defensive rotations, with players like Grigonis and Valanciunas leading the charge, while Hungary’s #14, Goloman, appears frustrated after a missed shot.

A rebound is grabbed, and commentators analyze the game, stating "physically there's no doubt they've been the stronger team, a plus 10 rebound advantage tells you all you need to know." segments.integrated_caption: The third quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds with Lithuania leading 62-38, as the clock ticks from 6:19 to 5:19. The segment opens with Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, dribbling up the court under aggressive pressure from Lithuania’s #40, Marius Grigonis, while the crowd's chants of 'Defense! Defense!' fill the arena. A commentator calls out a 'Three-pointer!' early in the sequence, followed by another noting, 'They get it back.' The action quickly transitions as Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, drives to the basket, drawing defenders, and passing out to set up a play. Commentators then shift their focus to a player named Sabonis, stating he's 'trying to get open' and is 'good on the three-ball,' bringing his total to '15 points in the game,' with one commentator praising him as a 'slick passing big man' with a 'terrific find.' The court is adorned with prominent branding including TCL, Colgate, Tencent, Tissot, JUNCKERS, WANDA, and Ganten. A referee's whistle sounds amidst the play. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, attempts a drive but is contested by Lithuania’s defense, leading to a turnover. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, then receives the ball and executes a powerful dunk, energizing the crowd. The commentators return to Sabonis, noting he 'grabs the board,' and one remarks that 'if he was shooting even a little bit better, the story of this game would be a lot different.' Later, a commentator announces a pass 'to Valanciunas,' who takes a shot '14 feet away,' but is 'unable to get that one.' The scoreboard updates reflect Lithuania’s dominance. The sequence concludes with Hungary’s #14, Goloman, visibly frustrated after a missed shot, receiving the ball at center court to inbound, while Lithuania’s #13 celebrates with a clenched fist, all under the watchful eye of a referee and the electric atmosphere of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 match, underscored by the prominent FIBA EuroBasket 2022 and TCL court logos.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with Hungary’s team huddled around their coach during a timeout, the scoreboard prominently displaying Lithuania leading 64-40 in the third quarter with 4:43 remaining, while upbeat, rhythmic electronic music plays alongside a general crowd murmur. The coach, seated in a black chair, is animatedly giving instructions, gesturing with his hands as players like #1 (Goloman), #5 (Rosco Allen), #6 (Keller), #8 (Adam Hanga), #9 (David Vojvoda), #11 (Szilard Benke), #15 (Ferencz), #21 (Czirják), #25 (Váradi), and #12 (Somogyi) listen intently, some leaning forward or nodding, with the on-screen text 'TIEMPO DE HUNGARY' confirming the break. As the score updates dynamically, the scene cuts to a wide view of the court, revealing the colorful FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court and prominent advertising for Smart, TCL, Tencent, and Ganten, as players from both teams reset. An announcer's voice, slightly muffled, twice declares "Get ready for the dance squad!" over the continuing music and crowd sounds. Lithuania’s #17 (Jonas Valanciunas) is then seen driving to the basket, drawing defenders and passing out, while Lithuania’s #40 (Marius Grigonis) defends aggressively. Following a defensive stop, the scoreboard updates to show 'FUERA DE BANDA' (out of bounds) for Hungary, and the camera focuses on Lithuania’s #9 (R. Giedraitis) preparing to inbound the ball. The upbeat music fades out as a loud whistle blows, and the crowd cheers intensely for a defensive play where Lithuania’s #19 (Zemaitis) blocks a shot, triggering the 'Mobil 1 TAPONES' graphic. A male commentator with an American accent then states, "So it's both teams making substitutions here," with a background shout of "Get up! Get up!" adding to the urgency. The commentator continues, describing the ball going "out of bounds" and confirming "they retain possession," before his analysis of a player's read on the play is cut short, leaving the atmosphere tense with ongoing crowd noise. Referees in black-and-white striped shirts monitor the action closely throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a close-up of a blocked shot, highlighted by the 'Mobil 1 TAPONES' graphic, as Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, attempts a drive to the basket and is rejected by a Hungarian defender, a play commentators laud as an 'exceptional Mobile 1 block' and 'lockdown defense at its finest,' noting the defender's ability to 'knock it away.' The scene transitions to a wide-angle view of the court, where Lithuania leads 64-40 in the third quarter with 4:42 remaining, featuring the vibrant FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court, flanked by prominent TCL and Nike branding. Amidst rhythmic crowd chanting and a whistle, Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, gains possession and dribbles up the court, passing to #5, Rosco Allen, who drives and scores a layup, reducing the deficit to 64-42. This successful drive is described by commentators as 'driving inside, up and in on the layup,' praising it as a 'perfect setup' that 'opens up a clear path to the bucket.' Lithuania responds with a fast break, led by #19, Zemaitis, who dunks powerfully, energizing the crowd. The scoreboard updates to 66-42 as Lithuania’s #17 hits a mid-range jumper. Commentators then describe another Lithuanian scoring play, mentioning a 'pass to Brazdeikis' and 'Lekavicius' taking it in for a layup, noting 'he's got six' and praising the 'pinpoint passing for the finish.' Hungary’s #14, Goloman, receives the ball at center court to inbound, looking frustrated after a missed shot. The camera follows the ball as Lithuania’s #4, Marius Grigonis, defends aggressively, rotating to contest a shot. The sequence ends with Hungary’s #25 appearing frustrated as Lithuania maintains control. Throughout, the court is surrounded by advertising banners for Tissot, Tencent, Wanda, JUNCKERS, and Ganten, with the referee in a black-and-white striped shirt closely monitoring play, all contributing to an intense atmosphere with a visibly engaged crowd and focused court lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The third quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game unfolds with Lithuania holding a commanding 68-44 lead over Hungary, as the game clock counts down from 3:57 to 3:00. The sequence begins with Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, driving aggressively to the basket, drawing defenders before passing out, while announcers comment on "Sabonis" securing his ninth rebound and scoring with "what an effort," also noting "Brazdeikis" has seven points. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, then gains possession and dribbles up the court, attempting to spark a comeback, as an announcer praises the "fantastic" ball movement leading to "good looks." Lithuania’s #4, Marius Grigonis, defends fiercely, rotating to contest shots and disrupt Hungary’s offensive flow. Commentary continues with "Keller" laying it up off the glass, followed by analysis on the importance of "low post scoring" for a balanced offense. Prominent court branding for TCL, molten, Ganten, J9.com, and FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logos are visible, with the scoreboard updating to reflect Lithuania’s dominance. Hungary’s #5, Rosco Allen, attempts a drive but is met with resistance. Later, Hungary’s #14, Goloman, appears frustrated after a missed shot, wiping his face before receiving the ball at center court to inbound. Lithuania’s #19 executes a powerful dunk, energizing the crowd, while Valanciunas also hits a mid-range jumper and a free throw with composure. The referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, closely monitors the play, signaling 'BALÓN ENTRE DOS' during a jump ball. As the shot clock shows "five on the clock," a long shot attempt is made but "doesn't drop." The crowd, dressed in vibrant colors, reacts to the action, and the atmosphere remains electric under bright arena lighting, with on-screen graphics displaying field goal statistics ('TIROS DE CAMPO'). The segment concludes with an upbeat, rhythmic background music playing over crowd cheers and a whistle.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a basketball missing the rim, as a commentator notes, "And misses it off the right side of the rim," accompanied by the sound of the ball hitting the backboard. Lithuania, leading 70-44 in the third quarter, quickly transitions into a fast break. Lithuania’s #4, Marius Grigonis, drives aggressively, drawing defenders, while a player shouts "Right here!" amidst the dribbling sounds. Grigonis then passes to Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, who scores a layup, prompting the commentator to exclaim, "Drives to the hoop. He gets that one!" A crowd cheer erupts, followed by a distinct buzzer sound as the scoreboard updates. Upbeat, rhythmic background music begins to play during a timeout. Hungary’s #12, A. Somogyi, inbounds the ball, with prominent court branding like the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, TCL, Smart, Tencent, Colgate, and Ganten visible. As play resumes, Hungary’s #14, Goloman, attempts a shot but misses, and Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, secures the rebound. A commentator notes, "Knocks it loose," followed by a referee's whistle and the call, "Foul!" The arena's PA announcer then declares, "First personal foul. First team foul," and later, "Checking in for the away team," amidst crowd murmuring. The Lithuanian team celebrates with high-fives and animated gestures, including Lithuania’s #31 roaring in triumph and #13 clenching his fist. The camera pans to the bench, showing Lithuania’s #8, #11, and #40 resting and hydrating. The game continues with Hungary’s #12 dribbling up the court, guarded by Lithuania’s #6, while the crowd remains engaged. On-screen graphics display player stats, including Marius Grigonis with 12 points and 100% shooting efficiency, and substitutions like A. Butkevicius and P. Brazdeikis are shown. The intense atmosphere is palpable, with the referee closely monitoring play. The sequence concludes with Hungary’s #12 attempting a pass under pressure, as Lithuania’s defense rotates to contest, maintaining their dominant position, punctuated by a player's shout of "Hey!" and continued dribbling sounds.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video captures a dynamic sequence from a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 basketball game between Lithuania and Hungary, with Lithuania leading 70-44 in the third quarter. The action begins with Lithuania in possession, executing a fast break as Lithuania’s #4, Marius Grigonis, drives aggressively toward the basket, drawing defenders before passing to Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, who scores a layup. The scoreboard updates to reflect the new score, and the camera follows the ball as Hungary’s #12, A. Somogyi, inbounds the ball after a timeout. The court is adorned with prominent branding including the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo, TCL court logo, and advertisements for Smart, Tencent, Colgate, and Ganten. As play resumes, Hungary’s #14, Goloman, attempts a shot but misses, and Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, secures the rebound. The Lithuanian team celebrates with high-fives and animated gestures, including Lithuania’s #31 roaring in triumph and #13 clenching his fist. The camera pans to the bench, where Lithuania’s players, including #8, #11, and #40, are seen resting and hydrating. The game continues with Hungary’s #12 dribbling up the court, guarded by Lithuania’s #6, while the crowd remains engaged. The on-screen graphics display player stats, including Marius Grigonis with 12 points and 100% shooting efficiency, and substitutions are shown with names like A. Butkevicius and P. Brazdeikis. The atmosphere is intense, with the referee closely monitoring play and the crowd’s energy palpable in the packed arena. The sequence ends with Hungary’s #12 attempting a pass under pressure, as Lithuania’s defense rotates to contest, maintaining their dominant position.

segments.integrated_caption: The third quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game between Lithuania and Hungary unfolds with Lithuania leading 70-44, amidst an animated crowd in red and green attire and rhythmic background music. Lithuania's #12, Varadi, drives aggressively, drawing defenders before passing to #22, Ellingsfeld, who scores a layup. The action intensifies with a commentator noting "It's three on three on the fast break" as Lithuania's #20, Brazdeikis, secures a defensive rebound and initiates a quick break, passing to #12 for another layup. A commentator exclaims, "And Brazdeikis gets it to go," adding that "Brazdeikis has got four points now in the quarter." The commentary then shifts to defensive strategy, with one analyst remarking, "How frustrating for the defense. You get the stop but then fail to block out," followed by another deeper voice emphasizing, "Yeah, and it's inexcusable. But all too common. Basketball one-on-one. When the shot goes up, put a body on someone." Hungary's #14, Goloman, attempts a three-pointer, which a commentator describes as "There's the three" and "No good there," with Lithuania's #4, Marius Grigonis, aggressively contesting. The ball is recovered by Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, who drives into the paint, drawing multiple defenders, and passes to #22 for a mid-range jumper, updating the scoreboard to 72-44. The crowd's energy is palpable with shouts of "We want game!" and "One more!" as Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, receives the inbound pass and drives, met by strong defense from Lithuania’s #6 and #22. The ball is passed to #14, who attempts a contested shot and misses. A commentator notes, "The rebound by Brazdeikis," adding that "Brazdeikis has got four rebounds now tonight." Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, secures the rebound and initiates a fast break, passing to #20 who scores. The court prominently features branding like TCL, Tissot, Nike, JUNCKERS, WANDA, and Ganten, with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court. A referee in black-and-white stripes closely monitors the play under bright, consistent lighting. The sequence concludes with Hungary’s #14, visibly frustrated after the missed shot, wiping his face as he receives the ball at center court to inbound, with a commentator stating, "1:27 left in the third."

The ball is recovered by Lithuania’s #17, Jonas Valanciunas, who drives into the paint, draws multiple defenders, and passes to #22 for a mid-range jumper. The scoreboard updates to 72-44. Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, receives the inbound pass and drives, but is met with strong defense from Lithuania’s #6 and #22. The ball is passed to #14, who attempts a contested shot and misses. Lithuania’s #19, Zemaitis, secures the rebound and initiates a fast break, passing to #20 who scores. The court features prominent branding including TCL, Tissot, Nike, JUNCKERS, WANDA, and Ganten, with the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 logo at center court. The crowd is animated, with fans in red and green attire cheering. The referee in black-and-white stripes closely monitors the play, signaling fouls and transitions. The lighting remains bright and consistent, highlighting the polished wooden court and vibrant sideline advertisements. The sequence ends with Hungary’s #14 receiving the ball at center court to inbound, visibly frustrated after the missed shot, wiping his face as the clock ticks down to 1:28 in the third quarter.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with Lithuania leading Hungary 74-44 in the third quarter of the FIBA EuroBasket 2022 game, with 51 seconds left on the clock. Lithuania’s #9, Brazdeikis, is seen focused on defense, while Hungary’s #2, Somogyi, dribbles up the court, guarded by Lithuania’s #22. Commentators reflect on a previous "big difference in points in the paint" between the teams. Hungary’s #12, Ellingsfeld, drives toward the basket, drawing Lithuania’s #6 and #51, but his layup is declared "off target," with a commentator urging him to "do a better job making the D pay for their mistakes." An on-screen graphic highlights Hungary's "0 puntos en 2:44" scoring drought. The ball is then passed to Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, who attempts a three-pointer from the right wing, but it's "off-line," leading a commentator to critique his "bad night from the field," suggesting it will "only get worse if he keeps throwing up shots like that." Lithuania’s #12 secures the rebound and swiftly initiates a fast break, driving for a layup, igniting the colorful crowd. The vibrant court, adorned with the 'FIBA EuroBasket 2022' and 'TCL' logos, is surrounded by 'J9.com', 'molten', 'Ganten', 'FIBA.basketball', and 'COLOGNE' branding. A referee in black-and-white stripes closely monitors the intense play, as Lithuania maintains control, while Hungary's "SEQUIA ANOTADORA" persists amidst the electric arena atmosphere.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures a dynamic and intense moment in a FIBA EuroBasket 2022 basketball game between Lithuania and Hungary, with Lithuania leading 74-44 in the third quarter. The action begins with a close-up of Lithuania’s #9, Brazdeikis, in a green and yellow 'LIETUVA' jersey, visibly focused and running back on defense. The camera then pulls back to a wide view of the court, showcasing the vibrant 'FIBA EuroBasket 2022' logo at center court and the prominent red 'TCL' logo. Hungary’s #2, Somogyi, in a white jersey with red and green trim, dribbles up the court, guarded by Lithuania’s #22, who actively defends. The play shifts as Hungary’s #12, Ellingsfeld, drives toward the basket, drawing defenders, while Lithuania’s #6 and #51 rotate to contest. The scoreboard updates show Hungary’s scoring drought, with a graphic indicating '0 puntos en 2:44'. The ball is passed to Hungary’s #11, Szilard Benke, who attempts a three-pointer, but it misses. Lithuania’s #12, in a green jersey, secures the rebound and initiates a fast break, driving to the basket for a layup. The crowd, a colorful mix of fans in red, green, and yellow, reacts with energy. The court is surrounded by various advertisements including 'J9.com', 'molten', 'Ganten', and 'FIBA', with the 'COLOGNE' branding visible near the basket. The referee, in a black-and-white striped shirt, closely monitors the play, signaling and positioning himself strategically. The sequence ends with Lithuania maintaining control, executing precise passes and defensive rotations, while Hungary struggles to break their scoring drought, highlighted by the on-screen graphic 'SEQUIA ANOTADORA'. The atmosphere is electric, with the bright arena lights and animated crowd adding to the intensity of the game.
